fettuccine-alfredo.name: Fettuccine Alfredo
instructions: Cook 12 oz. fettuccine or other long pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water over high heat, stirring occasionally, until very al dente. Drain, reserving 2 cups pasta cooking liquid.

Transfer 1 cup pasta cooking liquid to a large skillet. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, then gradually whisk in ¼ cup (½ stick) unsalted butter, cut into small pieces, no more than 1 Tbsp. at a time, until melted. Whisking constantly, gradually add ¾ cup (75 g) freshly grated Parmesan cheese, making sure it’s completely melted and incorporated before adding more. Add cooked pasta and toss to coat, adding more pasta cooking liquid as needed, until sauce blankets noodles completely. Garnish with freshly ground black pepper and more Parmesan cheese. 

Editor’s note: This recipe was first printed in our October 2016 issue. Head this way for more of our best pasta recipes →

flatbread-with-smoked-trout-radishes-and.name: Flatbread With Smoked Trout, Radishes, And Herbs

ingredients.item: 0.5 garlic-herb naan or 1 pound store-bought pizza dough

ingredients.prep: trimmed, thinly sliced on a mandoline

instructions: Line a fine-mesh sieve with cheesecloth and set over a medium bowl. Place yogurt in sieve, cover with plastic wrap, and let drain in refrigerator at least 1 day and up to 2 days if you want it slightly thicker. Discard excess liquid. (Or skip this step entirely and use 1½ cups store-bought labneh instead.)

Prepare a grill for medium-high, indirect heat (for a charcoal grill, bank coals on one side of grill; for a gas grill, leave one or two burners off); lightly oil grate. Working with 1 piece of dough at a time, gently stretch to about a 10x8" oval. Grill over direct heat, turning and rotating as needed, until bread is stiff and both sides are lightly charred, about 3 minutes total. Move to indirect heat to keep warm while you grill remaining piece of dough.
Transfer both flatbreads to a work surface and spread drained yogurt over. Top with trout, radishes, dill, and chives. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and serve with lemon wedges for squeezing over.
Do Ahead: Yogurt can be drained 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.

flounder-poached-in-fennel-tomato-sauce.name: Flounder Poached In Fennel-tomato Sauce

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced, plus fennel fronds for garnish

instructions: A spice mill (optional)
Coarsely grind fennel seeds and red pepper flakes in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle. Alternatively, place in a resealable freezer bag. Crush with a rolling pin or large skillet. Set aside.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add sliced fennel, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring often, until soft, 10-12 minutes. Add spices and cook, stirring often, until spices are beginning to darken, about 1 minute.
Add marinara sauce and 1/2 cup water to skillet; bring to a simmer and cook to meld flavors, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

Season fish with salt and pepper and fold fillets in thirds or in half to form packets. Place fillets in sauce. Cover skillet and reduce heat to medium-low. Cook until fish is opaque and beginning to flake, 12-15 minutes.
Divide fennel-tomato sauce and fish among shallow bowls. Sprinkle tarragon and fennel fronds over.

fluffy-belgian-waffles.name: Fluffy Belgian Waffles
instructions: Meanwhile, whisk eggs until fluffy. Whisk in milk, oil, sugar, salt, baking powder, all-purpose flour, and whole wheat flour, whisking slightly after each ingredient is added in the exact order; do not over mix.
Pour ¾ cup batter into center of hot waffle iron, spreading with a metal knife if necessary.
Close iron; bake until it stops steaming, 2 to 5 minutes, depending on iron. Carefully remove waffle with a fork. Repeat with remaining batter.

folar-de-vouzela-teleculinaria.name: Folar De Vouzela
instructions: Mix the flour with sugar and a pinch of salt and have it on the kitchen counter. Open a cavity in the center, add the eggs, the lemon scrape and the melted butter, and slowly add the dissolved yeast into the warm milk.
When it's well connected, form a ball with the dough, put it in a bowl sprinkled with flour and let it leave until it doubles volume.
Divide the dough into two equal parts, extend them with the roll, brush them with butter, sprinkle them with sugar and wrap them around so that the sprinkled part is inside.

Give the horseshoe shape (in u) to the masses, let them leave a little longer and then dispose of them on a board, lined with vegetable paper. Brush with beaten egg, sprinkle again with sugar and take it to the oven, preheated to 180°C, for about 35 minutes.

fresh-fruit-tart-with-almond-press-in-cr.name: Fresh Fruit Tart With Almond Press-in Crust

ingredients.item: Almond flour (we like bob’s red mill super-fine natural almond flour since it includes the skins for extra flavor
ingredients.prep: but you can sub with  0.5 cup all-purpose flour)

ingredients.item: . vanilla paste or 1 0.5 tsp. vanilla extract

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Whisk all-purpose flour, almond flour, sugar, and salt in a medium bowl. Whisk egg yolks and 1 Tbsp. cold water in a small bowl.

Add butter to dry ingredients. Rub butter into dry ingredients with your fingertips to create large, shaggy pieces of dough (the idea is to smash the butter into the flour, creating some pieces that are flat and thin and some that are large and irregular).

Using a fork or your fingertips, toss egg yolk mixture into dough to combine. Knead dough in bowl with your hands until it starts to come together (it will still look a little dry; if it looks too dry and doesn't come together, add 1–2 Tbsp. more water, but only if needed).

Turn dough out onto a work surface and knead 1 or 2 more times. Divide dough in half, stack 1 piece on top of the other, then press down firmly to smash both dough pieces together. Repeat several times until dough is well combined and becomes slightly sticky.
Press dough into bottom and 1" up sides of a 9" springform pan or a 9" tart mold. Prick dough all over with a fork. Freeze until very firm, 20–30 minutes.

Bake crust until deep golden brown all over, 35–40 minutes. Let cool.
Do Ahead: Crust can be baked 2 days ahead. Wrap very tightly with plastic and store at room temperature.
Bring milk and vanilla paste to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Remove from heat.

Meanwhile, whisk egg yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a medium bowl (start slowly—it won’t want to combine easily at first). Then vigorously whisk until mixture lightens in color to a very pale yellow and is very thick, about 1 minute (the sugar should be nearly dissolved). Slowly whisk in hot milk mixture. Return custard base to saucepan. Heat over medium and bring to a simmer, whisking constantly, until mixture is bubbling and thick with no cornstarch taste, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat. Add butter a couple of pieces at a time, whisking until fully melted before adding more, until all butter is incorporated. Transfer to a medium bowl and cover with plastic wrap, pressing directly against surface of cream. Let cool.

Vigorously whisk cooled custard until smooth and slightly loosened. Spread inside cooled crust. Top with berries.
Do Ahead: Custard can be made 4 days ahead. Keep chilled.

fresh-nectarine-cake-with-blackberries.name: Fresh Nectarine Cake With Blackberries

ingredients.prep: halved, and pitted, or more to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9-inch springform pan.
Beat butter in a large bowl with an electric mixer until creamy. Add sugar and salt gradually and mix in well. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.

Combine flour and baking powder, sift, and quickly beat into the batter on low speed. Thin batter with a little milk if needed. Pour batter into the prepared springform pan. Arrange nectarines and blackberries on top in a decorative pattern.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 45 minutes. Dust with confectioners' sugar and cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Run a table knife around the edges to loosen. Invert carefully onto a serving plate or cooling rack. Let cool completely.

fresh-tuna-sandwich-teleculinaria.name: Fresh Tuna Sandwich
instructions: Cook the eggs in water with salt for 12 minutes after the water begins to boil. Drain, let it cool and peel.
Drain the tuna from the olive oil and put it in a bowl. Add mayonnaise, 1 chopped boiled egg and pickles, previously crushed, and mix until a paste is formed.
Bar half the slices of bread with the previous paste and fill with slices of the other boiled eggs and some arugula. Cover with the remaining slices of bread, hold with sticks and serve.

fried-cod-with-onion-recipe-teleculinari.name: Fried Cod With Onion Recipe
fried-cod-with-onion-recipe-teleculinari.original_name: Receita de bacalhau frito de cebolada
instructions: Peel and cut the onions into thin half-moons. Peel and lick your garlic teeth.
Light a pot with the olive oil, the blond, the onion and garlic and a pinch of salt and pepper. Let it cook in low heat.
Wipe the cod off big spines, pass it through flour and fry it in olive oil oil it's golden. Serves with the onion.

frozen-margaritas.name: Frozen Margaritas
instructions: Fill a blender with crushed ice.
Pour in limeade concentrate, tequila, and triple sec; blend until smooth.
Pour into margarita glasses and serve with lime wedges if desired.

frozen-pear-and-pink-peppercorn-mignonet.name: Frozen Pear And Pink Peppercorn Mignonette
instructions: Toast pink peppercorns in a small dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 1 minute. Let cool, then grind in a spice mill or finely chop.
Bring sugar and 1 cup water to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar. Add pear and cook until soft, about 3 minutes; drain pear.
Purée pear, vinegar, and 1/2 cup water in a blender until smooth. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a medium bowl, pressing on solids. Mix in pink peppercorns and salt.

Transfer mixture to a loaf pan and freeze 1 hour. Scrape mixture with a fork to break up frozen portions. Freeze, scraping and breaking up mixture every 30 minutes, until it resembles fluffy shaved ice, 2–4 hours.
DO AHEAD: Granita can be made 5 days ahead. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and keep frozen. Scrape before serving. (Serve alongside oysters.)

frozen-saganaki-shrimps-argiro.name: Frozen Saganaki Shrimps
instructions: We keep the shripp in the shell.
Clean the finocchio and cut it into thin slices. Fine chop the hot pepper.
Heat a large pan in a high heat.
Add the olive oil and sauté the flint until caramelized and golden. That way he'll take out all his sweetness and his perfumes.
Salt and pepper the shripp and sprinkle with a few drops of olive oil. Add them to the Caramelized finocchio and sauté for 1 minute on each side until red.
Add the hot pepper and ice with the ouzo. Let the alcohol Evaporate complete.
Lower the fire to very low.
In a bowl, dissolve the mustard with lemon juice and pour the shripp. Mix until dressed with mustard and lemon sauce.
Once the sauce is built, remove the pan from the heat. If you want more thin and juicy sauce, add a little water.
Sprinkle with finely chopped flints and serve immediately.
